title: Codify kubeflow ambient dataplane label in IaC (prevent terraform revert)
description: PoC fixed pipelines live by relabeling kubeflow ns istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient and removing istio-injection=enabled. This is NOT in IaC: kubeflow/terraform/main.tf:9 still sets istio-injection=enabled, so the next terraform apply will revert the label and re-break the pipelines UI (and ambient enrollment). Codify: (1) main.tf:9 -> istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient, drop istio-injection; (2) configure kubeflow profile-controller to label profile namespaces (kubeflow-admin + future) dataplane-mode=ambient; (3) merge the ml-pipeline-ui oauth2-proxy-principal policy fix (branch fix-pipelines-permission) to main and deploy. Verify pipelines still load after a terraform apply.
acceptance_criteria: terraform apply leaves kubeflow (and profile) namespaces labeled dataplane-mode=ambient; pipelines UI loads with the restrictive ml-pipeline-ui policy; no manual kubectl label needed.
notes: DONE (code, not yet merged/applied): (1) kubeflow/terraform/main.tf — kubeflow ns label istio-injection=enabled -> istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient (matches the live PoC that fixed pipelines). (2) kubeflow/charts/kubeflow-profiles/templates/configmaps.yaml — added istio.io/dataplane-mode: ambient to profiles-namespace-labels-data so profile namespaces (kubeflow-admin + future) join ambient. Both validated (terraform fmt clean; helm template renders). REMAINING: (a) merge the ml-pipeline-ui oauth2-proxy-principal policy fix from branch fix-pipelines-permission into main and deploy together with these; (b) profile-controller only adds labels, doesn't remove — existing kubeflow-admin will gain dataplane-mode=ambient but keep stale istio-injection=enabled (inert on this cluster since the sidecar injector isn't injecting; verify after deploy, relabel kubeflow-admin manually if needed); (c) profile namespaces still need waypoints for their L7 ns-owner-access-istio policy = platform-xd4 (notebooks/serving); (d) after terraform apply, confirm kubeflow ns keeps dataplane-mode=ambient and pipelines still load.

title: Fix ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy: allow oauth2-proxy SA, not ingress gateway
description: Pipelines UI returns Istio 'RBAC: access denied'. The ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy (authorization_policies.yaml:54-57) only allows cluster.local/ns/ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway, but oauth2-proxy is an in-path reverse proxy (reverse_proxy=true) that upstreams /pipeline/ directly to ml-pipeline-ui (oauth2-proxy configmap.yaml:44). The real direct caller is cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy. The wildcard-host ml-pipeline-ui VirtualService never wins over oauth2-proxy's exact dashboard_host VS. Fix: restrict source principals to the oauth2-proxy SA (mirrors the working kubeflow-dashboard policy) and correct the misleading comment. The /pipeline-straight-from-gateway assumption in the comment is wrong.
acceptance_criteria: ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy allows cluster.local/ns/<kubeflow-ns>/sa/oauth2-proxy; pipelines UI loads without 'RBAC: access denied'.
notes: PoC SUCCESS 2026-06-22 on dev: relabeled kubeflow ns istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient (removed istio-injection=enabled), rollout-restarted oauth2-proxy/ml-pipeline-ui/ml-pipeline. Restored ml-pipeline-ui policy to restrictive principal cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy. In-cluster test: caller SA=oauth2-proxy -> HTTP 200 (allowed); caller SA=default -> connection reset/curl 56 (denied). Confirms clean ambient enrollment fixes L4 principal matching AND the restrictive policy is properly enforced (allow-all regression resolved). CAVEAT: live ns-label change is NOT in IaC — kubeflow/terraform/main.tf:9 still sets istio-injection=enabled, so next terraform apply reverts it. Must codify: (1) terraform main.tf:9 -> dataplane-mode=ambient; (2) profile-controller to label profile namespaces ambient; (3) the oauth2-proxy-principal policy fix (committed on branch fix-pipelines-permission). Still TODO for full ambient: other kubeflow namespaces, waypoints for L7 policies (ml-pipeline x-auth-request-email, profile ns-owner-access-istio), Gateway API CRDs, profile-controller gateway SA fix (platform-7d1).

title: Fix ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy: allow ingress gateway SA (was oauth2-proxy)
description: Pipelines tab showed 'RBAC: access denied'. Root cause: earlier this session (platform-jk3) the ml-pipeline-ui Istio AuthorizationPolicy was restricted to sa/oauth2-proxy, but the /pipeline route is sent directly from the ingress gateway to ml-pipeline-ui (ml-pipeline-ui VirtualService), so the real caller is cluster.local/ns/ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway. ztunnel (ambient mesh) enforced the policy and denied the gateway. Fixed: live-patched the policy + updated kubeflow/charts/pipelines (values ingress.sa=istio-ingressgateway; authorization_policies.yaml principal). Verified gateway->/pipeline returns 200. Vindicates the original reviewer suggestion (gateway SA), which platform-jk3 wrongly overrode with oauth2-proxy.
close_reason: Live-patched ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy to allow cluster.local/ns/ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway; verified gateway->/pipeline/apis returns HTTP 200 (was 403 'RBAC: access denied'). Made permanent in kubeflow/charts/pipelines: values.yaml ingress.sa=istio-ingressgateway; authorization_policies.yaml ml-pipeline-ui principal uses {{ .Values.ingress.namespace }}/sa/{{ .Values.ingress.sa }}. helm template matches live value; helm lint clean. Corrects platform-jk3 (oauth2-proxy was the wrong caller; gateway routes /pipeline directly to ml-pipeline-ui).

title: Drop kube_data_auth_enabled: source provider host/CA from eks outputs + always exec
description: The kube_data_auth_enabled flag can't be correct for both greenfield (needs false) and deployed (needs true to refresh). Rewrite common/kube-provider.hcl + helm-provider.hcl to use var.cluster_endpoint + base64decode(var.cluster_certificate_authority_data) from eks module outputs (mockable) + always exec for token; remove the aws_eks_cluster data source and the flag. Add cluster_endpoint/cluster_certificate_authority_data vars+inputs to 10 consumers; remove orphan flag var from ebs-csi/efs-csi; strip TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled from Makefile + 3 workflows.
close_reason: Rewrote common/kube-provider.hcl + helm-provider.hcl to use var.cluster_endpoint + base64decode(var.cluster_certificate_authority_data) (from eks module outputs, mockable) + always exec; removed the aws_eks_cluster data source and kube_data_auth_enabled. Added cluster_endpoint/cluster_certificate_authority_data vars+inputs to 10 consumers; removed orphan flag var from ebs-csi/efs-csi; stripped TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled from Makefile + 3 workflows. Verified: make plan-all MAKE_EXIT=0, 0 cluster-unreachable errors, 0 hard errors (refreshes applied addons + plans new). fmt + YAML clean.

title: Relocate AWS LB Controller ServiceMonitor to Prometheus side (fix CRD ordering)
description: awslb-controller helm release set serviceMonitor.enabled=true, but it applies before prometheus (which ships the monitoring.coreos.com CRDs), so apply fails with 'no matches for kind ServiceMonitor; ensure CRDs are installed first'. Chart couples metrics Service to the ServiceMonitor. Fix: disable on LB chart; add prometheus/charts/lb-controller-monitor (metrics Service in kube-system + ServiceMonitor labeled release=prometheus) wired via helm_release depends_on prometheus.
close_reason: Disabled serviceMonitor on awslb-controller chart (removed the 2 set entries + orphaned monitoring_namespace var/input); added prometheus/charts/lb-controller-monitor (metrics Service in kube-system + ServiceMonitor labeled release=prometheus) wired via helm_release.lb_controller_monitor depends_on prometheus. Verified: original 'no matches for kind ServiceMonitor' error gone; awslb renders no ServiceMonitor; helm lint clean; isolated prometheus plan = 6 to add, 0 errors incl lb_controller_monitor; fmt clean.

title: Migrate Terragrunt CLI flags for 0.86.2 (Makefile + CI)
description: Terragrunt 0.86.2 removed old --terragrunt-* flags and run-all; make plan-all fails with 'flag provided but not defined: -terragrunt-non-interactive'. Migrate Makefile + 3 workflows: run-all <cmd> -> <cmd> --all; --terragrunt-non-interactive -> --non-interactive; --terragrunt-include-dir -> --queue-include-dir; --terragrunt-strict-include -> --queue-strict-include; hclfmt --terragrunt-check --terragrunt-diff -> hcl fmt --check --diff.
close_reason: Migrated Terragrunt 0.86.2 CLI flags in Makefile (~18 lines) and 3 CI workflows: run-all <cmd> -> <cmd> --all; --terragrunt-non-interactive -> --non-interactive; --terragrunt-include-dir -> --queue-include-dir; --terragrunt-strict-include -> --queue-strict-include; hclfmt --terragrunt-check --terragrunt-diff -> hcl fmt --check --diff. Verified: make plan-all exits 0, 15 units planned, 0 flag/hard errors. YAML valid. Root terragrunt.hcl->root.hcl deferred to platform-amv.

title: Restrict ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy to oauth2-proxy SA
description: ml-pipeline-ui istio AuthorizationPolicy was loosened to allow-all (- {}) in c5b8f6b, letting any in-mesh pod call the UI and forge the x-auth-request-email identity header (bypassing DEX). Restrict to the oauth2-proxy SA, matching sibling dashboard/profiles-kfam policies. Audited the other two allow-all policies (metadata-grpc-service, service-cache-server): both have out-of-mesh/sidecar-less callers, so allow-all is required there.
close_reason: Restricted ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy to cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy (mirrors dashboard/profiles); removed orphaned ingress.sa value. helm template + lint clean. metadata-grpc-service & service-cache-server kept allow-all (out-of-mesh callers); follow-up filed.

title: Remove hardcoded personal email/domain from charts and kiali module
description: Privacy review found admin@aedenjameson.com / aedenjameson.com baked into committed defaults. Set kubeflow-profiles and oauth2-proxy chart defaults to empty; externalize kiali root_domain to a variable wired from the account module.
close_reason: Set kubeflow-profiles/oauth2-proxy chart admin defaults to empty; externalized kiali root_domain to a variable wired from account module. grep clean, hclfmt+fmt clean.

title: Restore source-principal restriction on ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy
description: Security review finding (MEDIUM). kubeflow/charts/pipelines/templates/authorization_policies.yaml:50-54 changed the ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy rules from a principal restriction (only istio-ingressgateway SA) to allow-all (- {}). ml-pipeline-ui runs with ENABLE_AUTHZ=false and holds MinIO artifact creds in env, so the Istio policy is the only control on who can reach it. Allow-all lets any in-mesh workload reach the UI proxy directly (lateral movement / artifact disclosure). main has the secure form; the weakening is uncommitted on update-operator. Fix: revert rules to the from/source/principals block restricting to cluster.local/ns/<ingress-ns>/sa/<ingress-sa>.
acceptance_criteria: ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy rules restrict source principals to the istio-ingressgateway SA; no '- {}' allow-all remains on that policy.

title: Deploy waypoint proxies + Gateway API CRDs for L7 Kubeflow AuthorizationPolicies
description: In ambient mode ztunnel silently drops L7 rules (request.headers[x-auth-request-email], paths, methods). Pipelines work without waypoints because their effective rules are L4, but notebooks/KServe rely on the profile-controller ns-owner-access-istio policy whose L7 rules are dropped -> access will break. Also ml-pipeline's x-auth-request-email rule is dropped (currently masked by its L4 principal rule). Install Gateway API CRDs (currently absent), deploy waypoint proxies for the namespaces/workloads that need L7 authz (ml-pipeline, profile namespaces), and bind with istio.io/use-waypoint. Depends on the ambient labeling work.
acceptance_criteria: Gateway API CRDs installed; waypoints enforce L7 rules for ml-pipeline and profile ns-owner-access-istio; notebooks load for the namespace owner and are denied for non-owners.
notes: DEFERRED 2026-06-22: blocked on consistent ambient dataplane + no notebooks deployed to validate. Prereqs before revisiting: (1) all Kubeflow namespaces consistently ambient (kubeflow-admin currently runs SIDECARS, 2/2 pods); (2) profile-controller must stop setting istio-injection=enabled (conflicts with dataplane-mode=ambient); (3) platform-7d1 gateway-SA fix in ns-owner-access-istio; (4) a notebook/Notebook-CRD deployed to test owner-vs-nonowner. Gateway API CRDs absent (cluster-wide install needed). Istio 1.27 supports waypoints.

title: Fix MySQL upgrade crash-loop on existing PVCs (rename mysql PVC for clean reset)
description: PR feedback on KFP 2.16.1 upgrade: mysql datadir changed to /var/lib/mysql with no subPath, so on clusters with the existing mysql-pv-claim (data under mysql-8.0.26/ subdir) MySQL 8.4 refuses to init (non-empty datadir) and crash-loops. Decision: treat DB as greenfield (no data preservation). Rename PVC mysql-pv-claim -> mysql-pv-claim-v2 in pvc.yaml + deployments.yaml claimName so a fresh empty volume is provisioned (self-healing, no manual step), keeping upstream datadir layout. Document fresh-DB reset in README. Old mysql-pv-claim orphaned (delete to reclaim).
notes: Greenfield decision confirmed by user 2026-06-22. Related: platform-kin (the upgrade).
close_reason: Renamed mysql PVC mysql-pv-claim->mysql-pv-claim-v2 (pvc.yaml + deployments.yaml claimName), bumped chart 1.1.0->1.1.1 + TF pin so terraform detects the change. Deployed: new empty PVC bound, MySQL 8.4 inits cleanly (no crash-loop), Helm auto-prunes old PVC (gp2 Delete reclaims volume - no manual step). README updated. Sample pipeline SUCCEEDED on fresh DB (after restarting DB-consuming deploys to re-init schema; in a real version upgrade the api-server image change restarts it automatically).

title: Decide ServiceAccount strategy for single-user pipeline runs (default-editor in kubeflow ns)
description:
KFP 2.16.1 api-server runs in single-user mode (MULTIUSER=false), so pipeline runs execute in the 'kubeflow' namespace. The api-server default pipeline-runner SA is 'default-editor' (DEFAULTPIPELINERUNNERSERVICEACCOUNT in pipeline-api-server-config), but 'default-editor' only exists in profile namespaces (e.g. kubeflow-admin), NOT in 'kubeflow'. As a result, default pipeline runs in 'kubeflow' fail with: pods forbidden: serviceaccount "default-editor" not found.

During the 2.16.1 upgrade E2E verification (bead platform-kin), the sample pipeline only succeeded by overriding service_account=pipeline-runner at submit time. That is a workaround, not a default.

Decision needed - pick one:
1. Provision a 'default-editor' SA (+ appropriate edit RBAC / binding) in the 'kubeflow' namespace so default single-user runs work out of the box.
2. Change DEFAULTPIPELINERUNNERSERVICEACCOUNT to 'pipeline-runner' (already exists in 'kubeflow' with the pipeline-runner role) so the api-server default works without per-run overrides.
3. Move to true multi-user (run pipelines in profile namespaces like kubeflow-admin that already have default-editor + mlpipeline-minio-artifact), adjusting api-server MULTIUSER and routing accordingly.

Acceptance: a pipeline submitted with no explicit service_account runs to completion in the default namespace.
notes: Surfaced 2026-06-22 during KFP 2.3.0->2.16.1 upgrade E2E test on archegos-dev-eks. Pre-existing config characteristic of the multi-user-derived chart run single-user; not introduced by the version bump. Related: platform-kin.

title: Adopt SeaweedFS as Kubeflow Pipelines artifact store (replace MinIO)
description:
KFP 2.16.1's upstream platform-agnostic-multi-user overlay replaced standalone MinIO with SeaweedFS (chrislusf/seaweedfs:4.00) as the default artifact store. Evaluate and migrate kubeflow/charts/pipelines from MinIO to SeaweedFS to match the upstream default.

Scope:
- Replace the MinIO Deployment/Service/PVC with SeaweedFS (Deployment + seaweedfs-pvc + service exposing :8333 S3 / :9000 / :8111 master, SA seaweedfs).
- Adopt the upstream profile-controller sync.py that provisions per-namespace SeaweedFS S3 credentials via the SeaweedFS IAM API (replaces the static mlpipeline-minio-artifact creds flow).
- Update artifactRepository in workflow-controller-configmap endpoint minio-service -> seaweedfs.kubeflow:9000/8333; update OBJECT_STORE_HOST / S3_ENDPOINT_URL env.
- Reconcile/retire the TF random_password.minio_access_key/secret_key wiring in kubeflow/terraform/main.tf (becomes vestigial).
- Plan data migration / fresh-deploy strategy (artifacts in existing minio bucket).

Decision context: during the KFP 2.3.0 -> 2.16.1 upgrade (bead platform-kin) we chose to KEEP MinIO to keep blast radius contained; SeaweedFS adoption is deferred to this issue. Raised 2026-06-22.
notes: Sibling of platform-oaz (Postgres migration). Both defer an upstream-default backend change out of the version upgrade. Depends on platform-kin landing first.

title: KFP 2.16.1: verify (helm lint/template, kubeconform, live deploy)
description: helm lint + template render; kubeconform/kubectl --dry-run; make deploy-kubeflow (destructive, confirm first); post-deploy pod/CRD/UI + sample pipeline checks.
notes: E2E VERIFIED on archegos-dev-eks: all KFP 2.16.1 pods Running; images confirmed (kfp-api-server/frontend 2.16.1, workflow-controller v3.7.3, kfp-driver/launcher 2.16.1); UI+api healthz 200; sample 2-component v2 pipeline ran SUCCEEDED (5/5, data passing + artifacts to MinIO). FIXES applied during deploy: (1) recreated mysql PVC (datadir /var/lib/mysql-8.0.26->/var/lib/mysql made old data unusable -> mysql 8.4 refused init); (2) added kfp-launcher 'providers' block pointing minio:// to minio-service (KFP 2.16.1 defaults minio:// endpoint to seaweedfs.kubeflow:9000). NOTE: single-user api-server runs pipelines in 'kubeflow' ns which lacks default-editor SA; used service_account=pipeline-runner for the test.
close_reason: E2E verified on archegos-dev-eks: KFP 2.16.1 stack healthy, sample v2 pipeline ran SUCCEEDED (driver/launcher 2.16.1 + Argo v3.7.3 + MinIO). Reconcile deploy clean (0 changed). Test workflows cleaned up.

close_reason: Chart.yaml appVersion 2.3.0->2.16.1, version 1.0.7->1.1.0; main.tf helm_release.kubeflow_pipelines version 1.0.2->1.1.0 (now matches Chart.yaml).

close_reason: config_maps.yaml: pipeline-install-config appVersion 2.3.0->2.16.1; argo doc-URL comments v3.4.17->v3.7.3; workflow-controller-configmap executor block gained securityContext (runAsUser 65532 + seccomp) per upstream 2.16.1. Kept minio-service artifactRepository endpoint (minio retained).

title: KFP 2.16.1: reconcile RBAC/services/SAs vs upstream
description: Diff templates/{roles,cluster_roles,role_bindings,services,service_accounts}.yaml vs upstream; add new RBAC verbs/resources for Pipeline/PipelineVersion CRDs and webhooks.

Added required kfp-launcher + metadata-envoy-configmap ConfigMaps and wired envoy-config into metadata-envoy deployment (new image needs external config). Metacontroller kept on cluster-admin binding (functional). Services/SAs/bindings already complete.

description: Add base/webhook pipelineversion mutating + validating webhook configs wired to a cert-manager cert (reuse self-signed Issuer pattern). Add RBAC for pipelines/pipelineversions if needed.
close_reason: Out of scope: KFP 2.16.1 platform-agnostic-multi-user overlay does not include pipelineversion mutating/validating webhooks nor Pipeline/PipelineVersion CRDs (those are behind a separate feature overlay). Confirmed via rendered baseline /tmp/kfp-2.16.1.yaml (13 CRDs: 8 Argo + 3 metacontroller + scheduledworkflows + viewers).

Verify with kubectl apply --dry-run=server -f crds/.

Metacontroller CRDs must match metacontroller v4.11.22 (up from v2.0.5). Keep scheduledworkflows/viewers as-is.

title: Upgrade Kubeflow Pipelines chart 2.3.0 -> 2.16.1
description: Re-vendor kubeflow/charts/pipelines from upstream KFP 2.16.1 platform-agnostic-multi-user manifests, keeping MySQL and local customizations (namespace templating, cert-manager cache cert, minio/mysql secrets, oauth x-auth-request-email, istio VS/AuthZ/DestinationRule). Plan: /Users/aeden/.claude/plans/quiet-wiggling-allen.md. Approach/DB/verify decided 2026-06-22: re-vendor baseline, keep MySQL, verify via live make deploy-kubeflow.
close_reason: KFP chart upgraded 2.3.0->2.16.1 and verified end-to-end on archegos-dev-eks. All 7 implementation subtasks done; sample v2 pipeline ran SUCCEEDED. MySQL+MinIO retained (Postgres->platform-oaz, SeaweedFS->platform-wiz deferred).

title: Migrate Kubeflow Pipelines DB backend from MySQL to PostgreSQL
description:
As part of (or following) the KFP 2.3.0 -> 2.16.1 upgrade, evaluate and migrate the pipelines chart database backend from MySQL 8.0.26 to PostgreSQL. KFP 2.16.1 has first-class PostgreSQL support (manifests/kustomize/third-party/postgresql and base/postgresql). This is intentionally scoped separately from the version upgrade to keep blast radius contained.

Scope:
- Replace the MySQL deployment/service/secret in kubeflow/charts/pipelines with a PostgreSQL deployment (or external RDS).
- Update api-server, cache-server, metadata-grpc, and persistence-agent DB config env (dbType/driver, host, port, secrets) for Postgres.
- Update pipeline-install-config and mysql-secret -> postgres equivalents.
- Decide on in-cluster Postgres vs AWS RDS PostgreSQL.
- Plan data migration / fresh-deploy strategy (terragrunt apply/destroy currently does fresh deploys).

Depends on the KFP 2.16.1 upgrade landing first (MySQL kept during that upgrade for minimal change).
notes: Raised during planning of the KFP 2.16.1 upgrade on 2026-06-22. The upgrade itself keeps MySQL; Postgres migration is deferred to this issue.

title: Make org_name an env-backed required input (ORG_NAME)
description: Reviewer: README listed ORG_NAME as an input env var, but the Makefile derived/exported it from org_name (default Archegos) and never read ORG_NAME as input; 'three required inputs' overstated. Chose to make the Makefile consistent: org_name ?= $(ORG_NAME); export ORG_NAME := ... (':=' avoids recursive-variable error); add require-org guard; require-deploy-vars + add-cluster depend on it. README table updated to all-three-required + export-once guidance.
close_reason: Makefile: org_name ?= $(ORG_NAME) (reads env, no default); export ORG_NAME := $(shell ... lowercase) using := to avoid recursive-variable error; added require-org guard; require-deploy-vars: require-org and add-cluster: require-org. Verified: org via param and via ORG_NAME env both work (lowercased, no recursion); missing ORG_NAME fails fast on deploy-* and add-cluster; make -n parses clean. README: table now all-three-required + env-backed, export-once recommendation, bare make plan-all/deploy-all.

title: Update README + add deploy-account/deploy-dex make targets
description: README was stale/broken (make plan-all example missing required ROOT_DOMAIN/ADMIN_EMAIL; no account/Dex/Kubeflow/domain coverage). Added deploy-account/deploy-dex (+destroy) Makefile targets wired into require-deploy-vars; rewrote README: required vars table, Route53 prerequisite, quickstart + corrected incremental order (account->vpc->eks->core addons->dex->prometheus->istio->kiali->kf addons->kubeflow), Authentication(Dex)/Monitoring sections, *.admin.<domain> access + dex_admin_password login, kubeflow addons+deploy, teardown, GH Actions vars/secrets, dashboard/ gitignore note.
close_reason: Added deploy-account/deploy-dex/destroy-account/destroy-dex targets (mirror deploy-prometheus, queue-strict-include) + wired into require-deploy-vars; verified dry-run renders correct terragrunt cmd and guard fires. Rewrote README: required-vars table, Route53 prerequisite, quickstart + corrected per-component order, new Account/Authentication(Dex)/Monitoring sections, *.admin.<domain> access + dex_admin_password login, kubeflow addons+deploy+dashboard URL, teardown, GH Actions vars/secrets, dashboard/ gitignore note. Verified: images exist, all 13 referenced make targets real, all 7 addon dirs exist, TOC anchors consistent.

title: CI plan step used kube_data_auth_enabled=true, breaking greenfield plan
description: infra-validate-plan Plan step set TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled=true, so the aws_eks_cluster data source in the generated kube/helm provider tried to read the mocked cluster (mock-cluster-name) and failed with 'couldn't find resource'. make plan-all works because plan-all sets false. Changed CI Plan step to false to match (apply/destroy keep true).
close_reason: Set TF_VAR_kube_data_auth_enabled=false on the infra-validate-plan Plan step (was true) so the aws_eks_cluster data source stays gated off during greenfield plan, matching make plan-all. apply/destroy keep true (cluster exists then). YAML valid.

title: CI: run Terraform (not OpenTofu) in infra workflows
description: infra-validate-plan/apply/destroy pinned tofu_version=1.9.8, but 1.9.8 is a Terraform version; OpenTofu has no 1.9.8 so mise 404'd. gruntwork-io/terragrunt-action has no tf_version input (only tofu_version/tf_path). Added hashicorp/setup-terraform (1.9.8) install step + tf_path: terraform on each terragrunt-action step so Terragrunt uses the terraform binary. Aligns CI with .tool-versions (terraform 1.9.8).
close_reason: All 3 workflows: added 'Install Terraform' step (hashicorp/setup-terraform@b9cd54a v3.1.2, terraform_version from env tf_version=1.9.8, wrapper off) after AWS creds; replaced tofu_version/tf_version action inputs with tf_path: terraform (satisfies action.yml mise-check and makes Terragrunt use the terraform binary; default tfpath is terraform anyway). env renamed tofu_version->tf_version. .tool-versions unchanged (still terraform 1.9.8). YAML validated. Not committed.

title: Centralize Route53 zone lookup in account module; pass root_zone_id to consumers
description: Option 2 from plan discussion: dex/prometheus/kiali/kubeflow each ran a live data.aws_route53_zone lookup keyed on root_domain, which fails during run-all plan on greenfield because account is mocked to mock.example.com. Move the lookup into the account module, output root_zone_id, and have consumers take zone_id from dependency.account.outputs.root_zone_id (mockable). Removes live route53 calls from 4 consumer plans.
close_reason: Moved route53 zone lookup into account module (data.aws_route53_zone.root + output root_zone_id); added mock root_zone_id; rewired dex/prometheus/kiali/kubeflow to consume var.root_zone_id from dependency.account.outputs.root_zone_id instead of their own live lookup. make plan-all now plans all modules cleanly: account does the single live lookup (Z0873790MAHSTW1NBQTC), consumers plan with mock zone_id (acm validation record known-after-apply). terraform fmt + terragrunt hclfmt clean.

title: Gate kubernetes/helm provider exec auth on kube_data_auth_enabled
description: Reviewer: exec (aws eks get-token) was always configured even when kube_data_auth_enabled=false, so the plan path (make plan-all, flag=false) forced get-token on refresh instead of falling back to kubeconfig. Wrapped exec in dynamic block gated on the flag in common/kube-provider.hcl + common/helm-provider.hcl; apply (flag=true via Makefile) keeps the exec/token-expiry fix. Fixed stale variable description (named removed aws_eks_cluster_auth data source) across 12 variables.tf.
close_reason: Wrapped exec in dynamic block gated on var.kube_data_auth_enabled in common/kube-provider.hcl and common/helm-provider.hcl (apply/flag=true keeps exec token-expiry fix; plan/flag=false drops exec -> kubeconfig fallback). Updated stale description naming the removed aws_eks_cluster_auth data source across all 12 variables.tf. terragrunt hclfmt clean; no aws_eks_cluster_auth references remain.

title: Fix misleading inbound-cidrs=0.0.0.0/0 comment on admin ALB ingresses
description: Security review flagged prometheus/terraform/main.tf:94 — the comment implies 0.0.0.0/0 is recommended, when it means the ALB is open to the entire internet. Replace/add an accurate comment across all four admin ingresses (grafana, kiali, dex, dashboard) that share one ALB group. Comment-only; value unchanged.
close_reason: Replaced misleading inbound-cidrs=0.0.0.0/0 comment (grafana, kiali) and added matching accurate comment (dex, dashboard) across all four shared-ALB admin ingresses. Comment-only; value unchanged. terraform fmt clean on all four modules.

title: Audit and tighten remaining allow-all (- {}) Istio AuthorizationPolicies in pipelines chart
description: kubeflow/charts/pipelines/templates/authorization_policies.yaml has additional allow-all rules (- {}): line 11-14, minio (line 35-38), and cache-server (line 142-145). These accept any in-mesh source. Likely pre-existing (separate from the ml-pipeline-ui finding in platform-nkx). Review each: determine the legitimate callers and replace - {} with from/source/principals restrictions where feasible, or document why allow-all is required. minio is highest concern since it serves artifacts with credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Each remaining '- {}' policy (line 11, minio, cache-server) is either scoped to specific source principals or has a documented justification for allow-all.

title: Standardize ingress gateway SA reference across kubeflow charts
description: Cross-chart inconsistency in how the ingress gateway service account is referenced: kubeflow-dashboard declares ingress.service_account=istio-ingressgateway in values.yaml but NO template uses it (dead key; its authz policy hardcodes sa/oauth2-proxy); oauth2-proxy hardcodes cluster.local/ns/{{ .Values.ingress.namespace }}/sa/istio-ingressgateway in its AuthorizationPolicy; pipelines (ml-pipeline-ui) now also hardcodes the same. Cleanup: pick one convention (e.g. ingress.service_account) and use it consistently across all kubeflow charts' AuthorizationPolicies, and remove the dead/unused key in kubeflow-dashboard. Surfaced during the ml-pipeline-ui authz fix (platform-aev).

title: Verify Istio ambient vs kubeflow namespace istio-injection=enabled
description: istio/system installs the ambient profile (ztunnel/cni, PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT), but the kubeflow namespace is labeled istio-injection=enabled (sidecar). Confirm whether kubeflow should be ambient (remove the injection label / add to ambient mesh) or whether sidecar is intended for those workloads. Surfaced during README update; out of README scope.
notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-22: Istio is deliberately installed in AMBIENT mode (istio/system/terraform/main.tf: profile: ambient, PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT: true, istiod+cni+ztunnel, Istio 1.27.0). The ingress gateway is a sidecar Envoy (gateway chart in ns 'ingress', istio-injection=enabled) which is correct for ambient. BUG: workload namespaces are mislabeled for the dataplane — kubeflow/terraform/main.tf:9 sets kubeflow ns label 'istio-injection=enabled' (sidecar, no-op here) instead of 'istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient'; kubeflow-admin (profile-controller-generated) is likewise istio-injection=enabled and has NO dataplane-mode label. Result: workloads are only partially/inconsistently enrolled in ambient, so ztunnel L4 principal rules don't match peer identity (ml-pipeline-ui 403, platform-720) and L7 rules are silently dropped (ml-pipeline x-auth-request-email; profile ns-owner-access-istio). Gateway API CRDs are NOT installed -> waypoints currently impossible. Direction: commit to ambient (matches IaC). Migration: (1) install Gateway API CRDs; (2) relabel kubeflow + profile namespaces dataplane-mode=ambient, drop istio-injection; (3) restart workloads to enroll cleanly (fixes L4/principal); (4) add waypoints for L7-policy workloads (ml-pipeline, profile ns-owner-access-istio); (5) fix profile-controller gateway SA ref (platform-7d1: uses istio-system/sa/istio-ingressgateway-service-account, real is ingress/sa/istio-ingressgateway).
close_reason: Verified: cluster is deliberately Istio ambient (istio/system/terraform: profile ambient); kubeflow/kubeflow-admin were mislabeled istio-injection=enabled instead of dataplane-mode=ambient, causing the broken enrollment. PoC confirmed relabel+restart fixes it. Remediation tracked in platform-iyr (codify IaC) and platform-xd4 (waypoints/L7).

title: Rename root terragrunt.hcl to root.hcl (Terragrunt anti-pattern warning)
description: Terragrunt 0.86.2 warns that using terragrunt.hcl as the root config is an anti-pattern that will become an error in a future version. Rename root terragrunt.hcl -> root.hcl and update find_in_parent_folders()/include references across all units. Warning-only today; deferred from the 0.86.2 CLI flag migration (platform-1hv).

title: Harden mesh authz for out-of-mesh Kubeflow Pipelines callers (metadata/cache/minio)
description: metadata-grpc-service, service-cache-server, and minio-service AuthorizationPolicies effectively allow-all (- {}) because their callers have no Istio identity: metadata-envoy and Argo pipeline step pods run with sidecar.istio.io/inject=false, and cache-server's mutating webhook is invoked by kube-apiserver (outside the mesh). A principals allow-list would 403 them. Properly restricting requires an architectural change: inject sidecars into metadata-envoy + step pods and enable STRICT mTLS via PeerAuthentication, then scope each policy. Out of scope for the ml-pipeline-ui fix (platform-jk3).

title: Remove or restore commented-out FSx Lustre / S3 block in kubeflow/terraform/main.tf
description: kubeflow/terraform/main.tf contains a large commented-out block: aws_security_group.fsx_lustre_sg, aws_fsx_lustre_file_system.fs, aws_s3_bucket.ml_platform, aws_fsx_data_repository_association.this, and helm_release.pv_fsx. Storage was migrated to gp2 PVCs (minio-pvc, mysql-pv-claim) and the kubeflow dependency was switched from eks/addons/fsx-csi to cert-manager. Decide whether FSx is being dropped (delete the dead commented code and confirm no remaining references) or restored (uncomment and re-wire). Don't leave commented-out infra in the committed branch.
acceptance_criteria: The commented-out FSx/S3 block is either deleted (with no dangling references to fsx-csi / pv-fsx) or restored and active; no commented-out resource blocks remain in main.tf.
